note_data.title: Marginal and absorption costing
keyPoints: **Marginal costing** treats only variable costs as product costs and fixed costs as period costs charged to profit and loss account
**Absorption costing** treats both variable and fixed costs as product costs absorbed into cost of goods sold
**Contribution** is sales revenue minus variable costs and is used to cover fixed costs and contribute to profit
**Break-even point** occurs when total contribution equals total fixed costs with zero profit or loss
**Margin of safety** is the excess of budgeted sales over break-even sales expressed as units or percentage
Marginal costing stock valuation includes only variable costs whereas absorption costing includes fixed production overheads
**Make or buy decisions** compare marginal cost of internal production with external purchase price
Profit reconciliation between methods occurs because fixed overheads in opening and closing stock differ between absorption and marginal costing
**Target profit** calculations require adding desired profit to fixed costs then dividing by contribution per unit

definitions.definition: The additional cost incurred by producing one extra unit of output, comprising only variable costs
A costing method that charges all production costs, both fixed and variable, to cost units
The amount by which sales revenue exceeds variable costs, available to contribute towards fixed costs and profit
The level of activity at which total costs equal total revenue and there is neither profit nor loss
The difference between actual or budgeted sales and break-even sales, expressed in units or as a percentage

howToAnswer.instruction: Show working clearly with formulas. Round to 2 decimal places unless stated otherwise.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this means that'
Show the difference in profits and explain with fixed overhead movements in stock

modelAnswer.question: Lahore Textiles Ltd has fixed costs of Rs 240,000. Variable cost per unit is Rs 15 and selling price is Rs 25. Calculate the break-even point in units and the margin of safety if budgeted sales are 30,000 units.
modelAnswer.answer:
Contribution per unit = Rs 25 - Rs 15 = Rs 10
Break-even point = Fixed costs ÷ Contribution per unit
Break-even point = Rs 240,000 ÷ Rs 10 = 24,000 units
Margin of safety = Budgeted sales - Break-even sales
Margin of safety = 30,000 - 24,000 = 6,000 units
Margin of safety % = (6,000 ÷ 30,000) × 100 = 20%
markBreakdown: Mark 4: Margin of safety units calculation
Mark 5: Margin of safety units answer

commonMistakes: Never include fixed costs in marginal cost calculations — only variable costs count
Students often forget to reconcile profit differences by considering fixed overheads absorbed in stock movements
In make or buy decisions, never compare total absorption cost with purchase price — use marginal cost only
Students incorrectly add profit to selling price instead of to fixed costs when calculating target profit
Confusing contribution per unit with gross profit per unit in break-even calculations
examinerTips: This topic appears in every Paper 2 and frequently in Paper 3
Always show break-even calculations as a formula followed by substitution
Profit reconciliation questions require clear explanation of stock valuation differences
Make or buy decisions must consider qualitative factors for full marks
formulas: Contribution per unit = Selling price per unit - Variable cost per unit
Break-even point (units) = Fixed costs ÷ Contribution per unit
Margin of safety = Budgeted sales - Break-even sales
Target profit units = (Fixed costs + Target profit) ÷ Contribution per unit
Profit under absorption costing = Profit under marginal costing + Fixed overheads absorbed in closing stock - Fixed overheads in opening stock
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Break-even chart showing total cost line, sales revenue line, break-even point intersection, fixed cost line, variable cost area, and margin of safety clearly labeled with Pakistani currency (Rs) on y-axis and units on x-axis
